---
title: "Getting Started"
sidebar: en
---

## Requirements

- An iOS, Android or desktop device.
- No server, no account, no sign-in.

## Setup

1. **Install.** Établi Vitrine is under development. Download the current **development build (signed APK)** from [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/etabli-dev/etabli-vitrine/releases) and install it on Android, or build for desktop from the repo. App Store, Google Play and F-Droid releases are not available yet.
2. **Open.** On first launch the app unpacks the bundled runtimes (WebR and shinylive) and brings up a local HTTP loopback server with the headers WebR needs for `SharedArrayBuffer`.
3. **Run the sample.** The library ships with a Shiny sample app; tap it to launch.

## Importing your own Shiny app

| Source | How |
|--------|-----|
| **File** | Import → file picker → either a `.zip` (shinylive bundle) or a folder with `app.R` (or `ui.R` + `server.R`). |
| **URL** | Import dialog → enter the URL of a `.zip` archive. |
| **Share sheet** | Share a `.zip` from another app and pick Vitrine as the target. |
| **Raw source** | Import dialog → "Raw source" → paste R code; Vitrine builds an internal shinylive `app.json`. |

Imported apps land in the library (the "display case") and can be relaunched or removed from there.

## Notes

- shinylive bundles are self-contained — all required R packages are inside the bundle.
- For raw source, Vitrine attempts to detect missing packages and reports any that are not available in the bundled WebR distribution.
- WebR and shinylive are open-source WebAssembly distributions; versions and source URLs in `THIRD_PARTY.md`.
